Sierra Regency

Roseville, Placer County, California

  • Independent Living

Contact & Location

Address
1015 Madden Ln, Roseville, CA 95661 · Map

About Sierra Regency

A gated independent retirement community in Roseville, established in 1988, with full-kitchen one- and two-bedroom apartments. Amenities include an indoor heated pool and spa, putting green, chapel, library, Sunday champagne brunch, and 24-hour staffing. Meals, weekly housekeeping, and transportation are bundled into the monthly rate.

Care offered here

  • Independent Living — what independent living includes and who it fits

Care needs usually grow over time. Ask every community what happens when your parent needs more care than their current level — moving twice is hard on everyone. See levels of care explained.

Lifestyle & Services

Every community sets its own rules on pets, couples moving in together, and age requirements — and its own menu of medical and non-medical services, including transportation. Ask for the current policies and a full service list in writing; details here can change and anything unlisted should be confirmed on your tour.

Ratings & reviews

We don't show a star rating ourselves — find it directly at the source:

  • California Department of Social Services, Community Care Licensing Division — state license & inspections
  • A Place for Mom — family & resident reviews
  • AssistedLivingCenter — family & resident reviews
  • Caring.com — family & resident reviews
  • Search for more reviews — Google

Do you work at Sierra Regency?

Families make one of their biggest decisions with pages like this one — and nobody can describe your community better than the people who work there. If you're on staff or manage this community, you can update these details, answer short topic-by-topic questionnaires (each takes just a few minutes), and share photos of your community. It's free, and accurate listings help the right families find you.

We know how demanding this work is, and this page isn't here to grade you — it's here to connect you with the right families. One thing families consistently want to know: how a community handles concerns when they come up. Our questionnaire gives you room to describe your process in your own words.

Claim & update this listing

Ratings & official records

We don't publish a star rating or numeric score for Sierra Regency ourselves — ratings change, and a wrong number here would be worse than none. Instead, here's exactly where to find the real ones, straight from the source.

State license & inspections

Who assigns it: California — not Medicare. Assisted living, memory care, and small care homes have no federal star rating; the state licenses and inspects them. What it covers: license status, inspection findings, and substantiated complaints.

Look up Sierra Regency at the California Department of Social Services, Community Care Licensing Division →

How to read the state record fairly

Family & resident reviews

Who writes it: families and residents, on public review sites. What it reflects: day-to-day life — food, friendliness, communication — not inspections.

  • Read Sierra Regency's reviews on A Place for Mom →
  • Read Sierra Regency's reviews on AssistedLivingCenter →
  • Read Sierra Regency's reviews on Caring.com →
  • Search for more reviews of Sierra Regency →

Whatever star average you find on those sites is a starting point, not a verdict. We don't write these reviews, verify what they say, or investigate the community ourselves — that's exactly why we send you to the source instead of showing you a number. Before you weigh what you find, know what it can hide:

  • Who writes them. Usually adult children, not the resident — so reviews capture communication, billing, and first impressions more than the texture of daily care. More →
  • Emotion moves the score. People post when they're delighted or furious; the quietly satisfied middle rarely does, and reviews written in grief, guilt, or relief carry that charge. The calm three-star reviews usually hold the real trade-offs. More →
  • Reviews can be solicited or self-reported. A community can fairly ask happy families to post — or cross the line by gating out unhappy ones, pressuring residents, or paying. A burst of five-star reviews in a single week is a flag, not a triumph. More →
  • Counts and dates matter. A 5.0 from three reviews is a coin flip, and new management makes a 2019 review irrelevant. Small homes gather few reviews by nature — lean on the state record and your own visit. Small homes →
  • Government stars are a different thing. Medicare's nursing-home stars aren't reviews at all — they come from inspections, staffing, and clinical data. How those work →

Read the full guide to reading reviews without being misled →

We link you to primary sources instead of showing a rating ourselves — why, and how to use what you find.

Other communities in Roseville

  • Oakmont of Roseville

    Roseville, CA

    • Assisted Living
    • Memory Care

    Oakmont of Roseville is a luxury assisted living and memory care community on Secret Ravine Parkway near Sutter Roseville Medical Center.

We are not a referral service and do not provide medical, legal, or financial advice — for decisions about care, consult the professionals involved. Facility details change; always confirm directly with a community. Last data review: 2026-07-11. Run a senior living community? Claim your free listing or see our marketing services for communities.
